info.title: Inventory Service
info.description:
Inventory is a Janis Service for managing the following entities Warehouses, Stock, Movements

tags.name: Infinite Stock
tags.description: Infinite stock refers to an unlimited inventory count for SKUs or a combination of SKUs and Warehouses.

tags.name: Movement
tags.description: A **Movement** is the action of transferring goods within the context of a warehouse.

tags.name: Order Receiving
tags.description: Order receiving involves processing inbound orders and adding them to a warehouse.

tags.name: Pickup Point
tags.description: Pickup points are designated locations where customers can collect their orders.

tags.name: Position
tags.description: Represents the physical spaces within warehouses where goods can be stored.

tags.name: Schema
tags.description: Refers to the organizational structure or schema of warehouses.

tags.name: Security Stock
tags.description: Security stock is an inventory buffer to ensure availability in case of demand fluctuations.

tags.name: Settings
tags.description: Configurations to ensure **WMS** operates according to the specific requirements of each operation.

tags.name: Shrinkage
tags.description: Represents inventory loss due to damage, expiration, or other reasons. Allows tracking and managing stock reductions.

tags.name: Sku Position
tags.description: Refers to the position of SKUs within a warehouse.

tags.name: Sprint
tags.description:
A **Sprint** groups a set of **Movements** in a warehouse. It can be generated either based on predefined limits in the **Warehouse** entity or specific movements, both requiring pending status.
See [Warehouse](/v2/service/wms#tag/Warehouse).

tags.name: Sprint Movement
tags.description: Refers to movements grouped under a specific sprint.

tags.name: Stock
tags.description:
Stock represents the actual quantity of a SKU in a warehouse, whether unpositioned or distributed across multiple positions. It comprises the following fields:
- `sku`: The SKU ID, referring to the `id` of the **SKU** Entity in the **Janis Catalog Service**. See [Sku](/v2/service/catalog#tag/Sku).
- `warehouse`: The Warehouse ID, referring to the `id` of the **Warehouse** Entity. See [Warehouse](/v2/service/wms#tag/Warehouse).
- `stock`: The number of real products available in the `warehouse` for the `sku`.

tags.name: Stock Control
tags.description: Allows the counting and modification of stock in a specific position.

tags.name: Stock Decision
tags.description:
StockDecision represents a decision made regarding specific stock units in the warehouse.
These decisions can be related to pre-shipment cancellations, post-shipment cancellations,
returns, or shrinkage. A StockDecision records the affected SKUs, quantities, and the actions
to be taken, such as marking items as shrinkage, relocating them within the warehouse,
or finalizing movements outside the system.

tags.name: Stock Decision SKU
tags.description:
StockDecisionSku represents stock decisions grouped by SKU, Warehouse and Position.
This entity provides an aggregated view of stock decisions, showing the total quantity
affected and the number of individual decisions for each unique combination of SKU,
Warehouse and Position, without considering the specific order context.

tags.name: Stock Movement
tags.description: Refers to the movement of stock within a warehouse.

tags.name: Stock Movement Motive
tags.description: Describes the reasons or motives behind stock movements.

tags.name: Stock Reservation
tags.description: Manages the reservation of stock for specific purposes or orders.

tags.name: Stored Good
tags.description: Refers to the actual physical items in a warehouse, which may include SKUs, order items, or order packages.

tags.name: Supplying
tags.description: Refers to the process of replenishing inventory.

tags.name: Warehouse
tags.description:
A **Warehouse** represents a physical or logical storage location where inventory is managed and fulfillment operations take place.

### Key Points
  - **`schemas`** → Defines the warehouse structure by specifying the areas used for inventory and order processing.
  - **`salesChannels`** → A list of sales channels supported by the warehouse. Orders from these channels can be processed within this warehouse.
  - **`tasks`** → Defines the operations the warehouse is allowed to perform. Possible values:
    - **`pickProducts`** → The warehouse picks products for order fulfillment.
    - **`sellsProducts`** → The warehouse directly sells products.
    - **`allowsInvoices`** → The warehouse is authorized to generate invoices.
    - **`shipsOrders`** → The warehouse ships orders to customers.
    - **`allowsPickup`** → Customers can pick up orders from this warehouse.
    - **`allowsDriveThrough`** → The warehouse supports drive-through order collection.

### **Warehouse Zones**

Each warehouse is structured into **zones**, defining where and how inventory is stored and processed.

#### **Structure of Zones**
The **organization of each zone** is determined by two key components:

- **`schema`** → Defines the structure of the zone, specifying how many hierarchical levels it has.
  - Example: A warehouse might be structured into **building, aisle, shelf**.
- **`mapping`** → Specifies the quantity of each level within the zone.
  - Example: **10 buildings, 3 aisles per building, 40 shelves per aisle**.

This allows for a flexible and scalable warehouse organization.

#### **Available Zones:**
- **`inbound`** → Manages the reception of SKUs, including unloading, verification, and initial sorting.
- **`slotting`** → Organizes SKUs in the warehouse for efficient storage and retrieval.
  - **`picking`** → Stores SKUs for direct order fulfillment.
  - **`storage`** → Holds SKUs before replenishment into picking locations.
- **`consolidation`** → Groups picked SKUs into packages or pallets before shipment.
- **`outbound`** → Manages the final dispatch of order packages or SKUs leaving the warehouse.
- **`returns`** → Handles client returns, including quality control and reintegration into stock. Items can be marked as **shrinkage** if they cannot be resold.
- **`holding`** → Temporary storage for SKUs awaiting further processing.
  - Includes unpicked items and misplaced SKUs.
  - **Shrinkage management** allows discarding unsellable or lost items.

post.description:
Assign a **Movement**.

Movement:
- Must be in `pending`, `started` or `picked` status and not assigned to a **Sprint**.
- When not assigned the permission `wms:movement:assign` will be validated
- When already assigned the permission `wms:movement:reassign` will be validated
- Will be assigned to `assigneeId` data field received or to **session** user

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The Movement ID. This refer to the `id` of the [Movement](/v2/service/wms#tag/Movement) Entity.

assigneeId.description:
This refer to the `id` of the **User** Entity of **Janis ID Service**. See [User](/v2/service/id#tag/User).

**Important** When no assigneeId was received will be assigned to **session** user.

post.description:
Cancel a pending **Movement**.

**Important**: Only the assigned user can cancel a Movement.

Movement:
- Must be in `pending` status and not be assigned to a **Sprint**.
- Will change to `canceled` status and add `dateCanceled`.
- The optional `reason` will be stored in the movement`history`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The Movement ID. This refer to the `id` of the [Movement](/v2/service/wms#tag/Movement) Entity.

reason.description: The reason why the movement is being canceled
reason.example: Movement canceled because its shipping was canceled

post.description:
End a **Movement**.

**Important**: Only the assigned user can end a Movement.

Movement:
- Must be in `pending`, `started` or `picked` status and not be assigned to a **Sprint**.
- Will change to `ended` status and add `dateEnded`.
- Will add `assigneeId`, `dateStarted` and `datePicked` when not present.

Reducing Quantity:
- `quantity` defines the final amount processed. If a lower quantity is sent, `reduceQuantityMotiveId` must be provided.
- Quantity cannot be increased.
- If the motive (**Motive** entity in the **Management** service) has the `createMovementWithRemainingQuantity` setting enabled, a new movement will be created with the remaining quantity. Otherwise, the remaining quantity will be ignored.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The Movement ID. This refer to the `id` of the [Movement](/v2/service/wms#tag/Movement) Entity.

post.description:
Pick a **Movement**.

**Important**: Only the assigned user can pick a Movement.

Movement:
- Must be in `pending` or `started` status and not be assigned to a **Sprint**.
- Will change to `picked` status and add `datePicked`.
- Will add `assigneeId` and `dateStarted` when not present.

Reducing Quantity:
- `quantity` defines the amount to pick. If a lower quantity is sent, `reduceQuantityMotiveId` must be provided.
- Quantity cannot be increased.
- If the motive (**Motive** entity in the **Management** service) has the `createMovementWithRemainingQuantity` setting enabled, a new movement will be created with the remaining quantity. Otherwise, the remaining quantity will be ignored.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The Movement ID. This refer to the `id` of the [Movement](/v2/service/wms#tag/Movement) Entity.

post.description:
Reject a pending **Movement**.

**Important**: Only the assigned user can reject a Movement.

Movement:
- Must be in `pending`, `started` or `picked` status and not be assigned to a **Sprint**.
- Will change to `rejected` status and add `dateEnded`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The Movement ID. This refer to the `id` of the [Movement](/v2/service/wms#tag/Movement) Entity.

post.description:
Reset a **Movement**.

Movement:
- Must be in `pending`, `started` or `picked` status and not be assigned to a **Sprint**.
- If `status: true` is received, the movement status will be reset to `pending`, and the fields `dateStarted` and `datePicked` will be cleared.
- If `assignee: true` is received, the `assigneeId` will be cleared.
- If `quantity: true` is received, the original movement quantity will be restored. This is useful when movement quantity was reduced in the Pick Api.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The Movement ID. This refer to the `id` of the [Movement](/v2/service/wms#tag/Movement) Entity.

status.description:
Define if the status must be reset to `pending`.

The status must be in `started` or `picked`.

quantity.description: Define if the quantity must be reset.

assignee.description: Define if the assignee must be reset.

post.description:
Start a **Movement**.

**Important**: Only the assigned user can start a Movement.

Movement:
- Must be in `pending` status and not be assigned to a **Sprint**.
- Will change to `started` status and add `dateStarted`.
- Will add `assigneeId` when not present.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The Movement ID. This refer to the `id` of the [Movement](/v2/service/wms#tag/Movement) Entity.

get.description:
It refers to the use the warehouse will have:
  - `inbound`: Manages the reception of SKUs into the warehouse, including unloading, verification, and initial sorting.
  - `slotting`: Organizes SKUs within the warehouse by assigning them to storage locations for optimal picking efficiency. It includes:
    - `picking`: The area where SKUs are stored for direct order fulfillment.
    - `storage`: The area where SKUs are stored before being replenished into picking locations.
  - `consolidation`: Groups and organizes picked SKUs into packages or pallets before shipment.
  - `outbound`: Manages the final dispatch of order packages or SKUs leaving the warehouse.
  - `holding`: A temporary storage area for SKUs that need further processing before being assigned a final location. This includes:
    - Items that were picked but later unpicked.
    - SKUs found misplaced or without a known location.
    - Shrinkage management, where items can be discarded if deemed unsellable or lost.
  - `returns`: Handles items or packages that are returned by clients, including quality control and reintegration into stock. It also allows marking items as shrinkage when they cannot be resold or restocked.

For more information abound Structure of Warehouse Zones see [Warehouse](/v2/service/wms#tag/Warehouse).

post.description:
This API sorts the items for a specific warehouse, based on the **Slotting** scheme configuration.

## Sorting Types

The sorting type is determined by the `pickingOrder` property in the schema configuration:

### 1. **By Categories** (`categories`)
- Applied when the schema has `pickingOrder: "categories"`
- Items are sorted according to the configured category list order

### 2. **By Stored Goods** (`storedGoods`)
- Applied when the schema has `pickingOrder: "storedGoods"` or when no `pickingOrder` is specified (default behavior)
- Items are sorted by the levels configured in the schema
- To consider storage positions it is necessary to activate the `useStoragePositionsInSorting` setting

### 3. **By SKU Positions** (`skuPositions`)
- Applied when the schema has `pickingOrder: "skuPositions"`
- Items are sorted by their specific positions in the warehouse

### 4. **By SKU Positions Then Categories** (`skuPositionsThenCategories`)
- Applied when the schema has `pickingOrder: "skuPositionsThenCategories"`
- First sorted by SKU positions (items with assigned positions), then by categories (items without position but with category), and finally items that cannot be sorted
- Zones are automatically assigned:
  - `darkstore`: Items that have an assigned position
  - `physicalStore`: Items without position but with category

## Special Behavior

> #### Important:
> - If the warehouse does not have a configured slotting scheme, no order will be applied to the items (returned as sent)
> - The `janis-simulate` header parameter allows simulating the sorting without applying real changes
> - The response varies according to the applied sorting type

requestBody.description: The list of **Items** to be sorted

post.description:
A sprint is created according to the following points
  - The user can send the movements of the sprint, otherwise the movements in `pending` status will be obtained.
  - The source and destination data of the sprint will be autocompleted with the movements warehouse details if available.
  - The amount of movements is determined by the sprint limits of the **Warehouse**. See [Warehouse](/v2/service/wms#tag/Warehouse).
  - Movements with committed dates are prioritized by date of creation of the movement or delivery.
  - The `assigneeId` will be the received by parameter or the user consuming the Api.

> Even though the deprecated fields are in use at this moment, all the data will internally be converted to the current data structure, so we encourage you to migrate to the new version.

requestBody.description: The data required to create a Sprint.

post.description:
Update the **User** assigned to a **Sprint**.

Sprint:
- must be in `started` status

Finish a **Sprint** and its **Movements** that has been started.

Sprint:
- must be in `started` status
- will change to `ended` status

Rejects one or many **Movements** of a Sprint that has been started.
When **Movements** are provided, **Sprint** wont be rejected, otherwise, sprint and it's active movements will be rejected

Sprint:
- must be in `started` status
- will change to `rejected` status

Movements:
- must be in `pending`, `started` or `picked` status

schemaType.description:
The schema type

- **`inbound`** → Manages the reception of SKUs, including unloading, verification, and initial sorting.
- **`slotting`** → Organizes SKUs in the warehouse for efficient storage and retrieval.
  - **`picking`** → Stores SKUs for direct order fulfillment.
  - **`storage`** → Holds SKUs before replenishment into picking locations.
- **`consolidation`** → Groups picked SKUs into packages or pallets before shipment.
- **`outbound`** → Manages the final dispatch of order packages or SKUs leaving the warehouse.
- **`returns`** → Handles client returns, including quality control and reintegration into stock. Items can be marked as **shrinkage** if they cannot be resold.
- **`holding`** → Temporary storage for SKUs awaiting further processing.
  - Includes unpicked items and misplaced SKUs.
  - **Shrinkage management** allows discarding unsellable or lost items.

unpickedOrderStoredGoodsBehavior.description:
Defines the behavior for handling StoredGoods related to an order after an unpick or a store change.

- `noReturn`: StoredGoods **will not** be returned.
- `returnToWarehouse`: StoredGoods **will be returned** to the Picking Warehouse.
- `returnToOriginalPosition`: StoredGoods **will be returned** to its original Picking position in the Warehouse.
  If the original position is not available, the `returnToWarehouse` behavior will be applied.
- `returnToHoldingPosition`: StoredGoods **will be returned** to a Holding Position.
  It will use a **SkuPosition** for each SKU or the first available Holding Position.
  If no Holding Position is available, the `returnToWarehouse` behavior will be applied.

canceledOrderStoredGoodsBehavior.description:
Defines the behavior for handling StoredGoods related to an order (before invoice) after cancellation.

- `noReturn`: StoredGoods **will not** be returned.
- `returnToWarehouse`: StoredGoods **will be returned** to the Picking Warehouse.
- `returnToOriginalPosition`: StoredGoods **will be returned** to its original Picking position in the Warehouse.
  If the original position is not available, the `returnToWarehouse` behavior will be applied.
- `returnToHoldingPosition`: StoredGoods **will be returned** to a Holding Position.
  It will use a **SkuPosition** for each SKU or the first available Holding Position.
  If no Holding Position is available, the `returnToWarehouse` behavior will be applied.

canceledInvoicedOrderStoredGoodsBehavior.description:
Defines the behavior for handling StoredGoods related to an order (after invoice) after cancellation.

- `canceledOrderStoredGoodsBehavior`: Use the same behavior as the `canceledOrderStoredGoodsBehavior` setting.
- `noReturn`: StoredGoods **will not** be returned.
- `returnToWarehouse`: StoredGoods **will be returned** to the Picking Warehouse.
- `returnToOriginalPosition`: StoredGoods **will be returned** to its original Picking position in the Warehouse.
  If the original position is not available, the `returnToWarehouse` behavior will be applied.
- `returnToHoldingPosition`: StoredGoods **will be returned** to a Holding Position.
  It will use a **SkuPosition** for each SKU or the first available Holding Position.
  If no Holding Position is available, the `returnToWarehouse` behavior will be applied.

- `started`. When the stock control was started by the Assignee

- `confirmed`. When all items are confirmed in stock control.

The current status of the stock decision:
- **pending**: The stock decision has been created but not yet confirmed. This is the initial state where the decision is waiting for confirmation before it can be processed.
- **confirmed**: The stock decision has been confirmed and is now ready to be processed. It indicates that the decision is finalized and can move forward with its associated actions.
- **processed**: The stock decision has been executed, and the necessary actions (e.g., stock reduction, relocation) have been completed and applied.

The default value is the ID of the same warehouse.
